questions.text: What does 'Al-Falaq' mean?
questions.answer: The Daybreak / The Dawn.

questions.text: Who are we commanded to seek refuge with?
questions.answer: The Lord of the daybreak (Birabbil-falaq).

questions.text: What is the first evil mentioned in Verse 2?
questions.answer: The evil of that which He created (Min sharri ma khalaq).

questions.text: What is the second evil mentioned in Verse 3?
questions.answer: The evil of darkness when it settles (Ghasiqin idha waqab).

questions.text: Who are the 'Naffathati fil-uqad'?
questions.answer: The blowers upon knots (referring to those who practice magic/sorcery).

questions.text: What is the final evil mentioned in Verse 5?
questions.answer: The evil of an envier when he envies (Hasidin idha hasad).

questions.text: What are 'Al-Mu'awwidhatayn'?
questions.answer: The 'Two Surahs of Refuge' (Al-Falaq and An-Nas).

questions.text: Why do we seek refuge in the 'Lord of the Daybreak' against darkness?
questions.answer: Just as Allah brings the light of dawn to remove the darkness of night, He can remove the 'darkness' of evil from our lives.

questions.text: What does 'Ghasiq' mean?
questions.answer: Darkness, the night, or the moon.

questions.text: What does 'Waqab' mean?
questions.answer: When it enters, spreads, or settles.

questions.text: How can one protect themselves from the 'evil eye' or envy?
questions.answer: By reciting this Surah and seeking refuge in Allah, and by saying 'MashaAllah'.

questions.text: Is this a Makkan or Madinan Surah?
questions.answer: Scholars differ, but many say it was revealed in Madinah when magic was attempted against the Prophet (SAW).

questions.text: How many verses are in Surah Al-Falaq?
questions.answer: Five verses.

questions.text: What does 'blowing on knots' refer to?
questions.answer: A technique used in magic where strings are knotted and incantations are blown upon them.

questions.text: What is the difference between Al-Falaq and An-Nas?
questions.answer: Al-Falaq focuses on external evils (magic, nature, envy), while An-Nas focuses on internal evils (whispers/waswas).

questions.text: Does Allah create 'evil'?
questions.answer: Allah creates everything; 'evil' is the harm that comes from some creations when they deviate or are misused, and we seek refuge in the Creator from that harm.

questions.text: What is 'Hasad' (Envy)?
questions.answer: Wishing for a blessing to be taken away from someone else.

questions.text: Why is the envier dangerous?
questions.answer: Because their malice can lead to harmful actions or the influence of the evil eye.

questions.text: When is it Sunnah to recite these Surahs?
questions.answer: After every prayer, before sleeping, and in the morning and evening.

questions.text: What is the primary message of Al-Falaq?
questions.answer: Complete reliance on Allah for protection from all forms of physical and hidden harm.
